description: This figure presents a temperature-time schematic illustrating the thermomechanical processing and subsequent annealing schedule for the investigated Fe-10Mn-10Al-0.7C low-density steel. The process route comprises two stages. First, the material is homogenized at 1200 °C for 1 h, hot-rolled with a finishing temperature around 880 °C, rapidly water-cooled to approximately 300 °C, and then air-cooled. Second, the hot-rolled plate is reheated to various annealing temperatures between 700 °C and 1200 °C (represented by multiple horizontal hold lines), held for 1 h, and finally water-quenched. Key labeled parameters include the 1200 °C homogenization, the 880 °C finish-rolling temperature, the 300 °C intermediate quench temperature, and the 700-1200 °C annealing window with isochronal 1 h holds. The caption states these are the hot-rolling and annealing conditions. This thermal history map defines the baseline processing route used to systematically investigate how annealing temperature affects microstructural evolution, phase fractions, and tensile properties across the studied specimens.
